perturbation.original_text: 1.27. "Territory" means worldwide, during the thirty six (36) month period following Notice of Initial Acceptance of First Product Order. Thereafter, "Territory" may exclude the People's Republic of China ("PRC"), to the extent that Integra has had no sales of Treated Integra Products in that country. In the event that Integra has had no such sales, PcoMed shall give sixty (60) days advance written notice of PcoMed's intent to utilize a third party to market the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology in the PRC .
perturbation.changed_text: 1.27. "Territory" means worldwide, during the thirty six (36) month period following Notice of Initial Acceptance of First Product Order. Thereafter, "Territory" shall always include the People's Republic of China ("PRC"), regardless of Integra's sales of Treated Integra Products in that country. In the event that Integra has had no such sales, PcoMed shall not have the right to utilize a third party to market the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology in the PRC .
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the definition of 'Territory'. The original text allows for the exclusion of the People's Republic of China (PRC) under certain conditions, while the changed text mandates that 'Territory' will always include PRC, irrespective of sales figures. This directly contradicts the conditions under which PRC could be excluded from the 'Territory', leading to ambiguity on the geographical scope of the agreement's provisions related to sales and commercialization rights. The contradicted text is at section 3.1 which is where it is detailed the Grant of Exclusive Rights. It does mention the phrase and creates In-Text contradiction: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, PcoMed hereby grants to Integra and its Affiliates a sole and exclusive worldwide right to sell and commercialize Integra Products treated by PcoMed
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. GRANT OF EXCLUSIVITY COMMERCIALIZATION 3.1. Grant of Exclusive Rights. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, PcoMed hereby grants to Integra and its Affiliates a sole and exclusive worldwide right to sell and commercialize Integra Products treated by PcoMed, with the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology (the "Right") for use in the Field in the Territory, including the right to conduct research and development in support of any of the foregoing. Nothing herein grants any rights to Integra (i) to manufacture any products using the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology or (ii) to sell or commercialize any products utilizing the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology other than the Integra Products for use in the Field in the Territory. Neither PcoMed nor its Affiliates shall sell or offer for sale, or grant rights under the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology to any Third Party in the Field in the Territory for products that utilize or embody the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology. Except as expressly stated in the preceding sentence, PcoMed shall not be subject to any restriction under this Agreement with regard to the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology. Without limiting the foregoing or Section 8.3 below, the exclusive nature of the Rights shall not in any way limit PcoMed from making, having made, using, selling or offering for sale products and/or services that do not utilize or embody the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original definition of 'Territory' by removing the possibility of excluding the People's Republic of China (PRC) under certain conditions. This creates a strong in-text contradiction with section 3.1, which grants exclusive worldwide rights within the 'Territory.' The original text in 1.27 allows for the exclusion of PRC, while the changed text forbids it. This affects the scope of exclusivity granted in 3.1, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes regarding commercialization rights in the PRC. The changed text therefore creates a direct incompatibility.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1. Confidential Information. Except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or otherwise agreed to in writing by the parties, each party agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and for 20 years thereafter, it shall keep confidential and shall not publish or otherwise disclose and shall not use for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement any Confidential Information furnished to it by the other party pursuant to this Agreement, except that the foregoing shall not apply to any information for which the receiving party can demonstrate that such information: (i) was already known to the receiving party, other than under an obligation of confidentiality, at the time of disclosure by the other party; (ii) was generally available to the public or otherwise part of the public domain at the time of its disclosure to the receiving party; (iii) later became part of the public domain through no act or omission of the receiving party; (iv) was disclosed to the receiving party by a Third Party who had no obligation to the disclosing party not to disclose such information to others; (iv) was independently developed by a person having no knowledge of or access to the disclosing party's Confidential Information; or (v) is an Authorized Disclosure under Section 5.3 below.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1. Confidential Information. Except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or otherwise agreed to in writing by the parties, each party agrees that, for the term of this Agreement and indefinitely thereafter, it shall keep confidential and shall not publish or otherwise disclose and shall not use for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement any Confidential Information furnished to it by the other party pursuant to this Agreement, except that the foregoing shall not apply to any information for which the receiving party can demonstrate that such information: (i) was already known to the receiving party, other than under an obligation of confidentiality, at the time of disclosure by the other party; (ii) was generally available to the public or otherwise part of the public domain at the time of its disclosure to the receiving party; (iii) later became part of the public domain through no act or omission of the receiving party; (iv) was disclosed to the receiving party by a Third Party who had no obligation to the disclosing party not to disclose such information to others; (iv) was independently developed by a person having no knowledge of or access to the disclosing party's Confidential Information; or (v) is an Authorized Disclosure under Section 5.3 below.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an ambiguity regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. The original text specifies a 20-year period after the term of the agreement, whereas the modified text extends this obligation indefinitely. This creates a direct conflict and causes uncertainty. A reference to how long the agreement lasts creates the In-Text Contradiction. It does not detail any duration of how long the agreement, the first sentence in Section 4.1 clarifies it's duration
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. TERM AND TERMINATION. 4.1. Initial Term. The initial term of this Agreement (the "Initial Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and shall end on the date that payment is due for Minimum Payment Period 7, pursuant to Section 2.4 hereof and as set forth in Attachment B hereof, unless earlier terminated as provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5.1 to impose an indefinite confidentiality obligation, whereas Section 4.1 specifies a finite term for the agreement. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the confidentiality obligations now potentially extend beyond the defined term of the agreement, causing ambiguity about the duration of obligations under the contract, as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2. Records and Audits. Integra shall keep and maintain accurate records and documentation pertaining to Net Sales of Treated Integra Product in sufficient detail to permit PcoMed to calculate payments due hereunder. Integra shall retain such records and documentation for a period that is consistent with its Records Retention Policy. Such records and documentation will be available for inspection during such period by an independent certified public accountant selected by PcoMed and reasonably acceptable to Integra, solely for the purpose of verifying the payments made by Integra under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2. Records and Audits. Integra shall keep and maintain accurate records and documentation pertaining to Net Sales of Treated Integra Product in sufficient detail to permit PcoMed to calculate payments due hereunder. Integra shall retain such records and documentation for a period of one year following the end of each calendar quarter. Such records and documentation will be available for inspection during such period by an independent certified public accountant selected by PcoMed and reasonably acceptable to Integra, solely for the purpose of verifying the payments made by Integra under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the retention period for records related to Net Sales. The original text states that Integra will retain records consistent with its Records Retention Policy, implying a potentially longer or indefinite period. The changed text specifies a retention period of only one year following the end of each calendar quarter. This contradiction raises questions regarding the actual duration for which records must be maintained. It makes PcoMed's ability to audit for an indefinite timeline difficult, if their records retention policy goes beyond one year of that calendar quarter. Section 6.1 mentions what is required in payments, and there is now an in-text contradiction as to how long they are required to maintain these records.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. PAYMENT AND ACCOUNTING. 6.1. Payment Terms and Reports. Payments due under Section 2.2 shall be payable to PcoMed by Integra on a quarterly basis within 45 days following the end of each calendar quarter. Each such payment shall be accompanied by a statement setting forth in reasonable detail (i) the number and type of Treated Integra Product and Partially Treated Integra Product sold and the Net Sales applicable thereto, (ii) a breakdown of all the components of Net Sales for the determination of payments due under Sections 2.2 (the numbers may be and type of products may be stated in the aggregate and not by customer and are not required to be detailed by geographic area unless Fee rates are different in different geographic areas). Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product shall be considered as being sold for the purpose of the calculation of payments due under Sections 2.2 when the payments for the Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product are received by Integra or its Affiliates from a Third Party. All payments to be made under this Agreement shall be paid in United States dollars. Net Sales of Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product and fees in currencies other than United States dollars shall be first determined in the currency of the country in which they are earned and shall be converted (for the purpose of calculation only) in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles for financial reporting in the United States.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 6.2 specifies a record retention period of one year following the end of each calendar quarter. This directly contradicts the original provision that Integra would retain records in accordance with its Records Retention Policy, which may have a longer duration. This creates an in-text contradiction because it limits PcoMed's ability to audit Integra's payments under Section 6.1 effectively, if Integra's Records Retention Policy dictates a longer duration. The change thus introduces ambiguity and conflicts with the requirements for payment and accounting, specifically affecting the detailed statement Integra must provide quarterly.
